title: Podman inoperable without writable $HOME
body:
### Issue Description

I am creating a sysuser without a home directory specifically to further isolate rootless containers using podman. I have changed the storage location using `/etc/containers/storage.conf`, making it based on the `USER` env var instead of `$HOME`. But I am unable to use podman at all unless I make `HOME` as an existing writable directory.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Create a user with subuid entries but no home:

    useradd --add-subids-for-system --system --home-dir /var/empty/newuser --shell /usr/sbin/nologin newuser

Edit `/etc/containers/storage.conf`:

    rootless_storage_path = "/var/local/containers/storage/$USER"

As that user:

    podman info # fails
    mkdir /tmp/abc
    HOME=/tmp/abc podman run --rm -it fedora

### Describe the results you received

Commands without specifying `HOME` as a writable path, even `info`, fail entirely:

    cannot resolve /var/empty/newuser: lstat /var/empty/newuser: no such file or directory

Specifying `HOME` succeeds. For `podman run`, it creates a `$HOME/.cache/containers/short-name-aliases.conf.lock`

### Describe the results you expected

I would like `podman info` (and other commands checking for the user's local conf file) to work without the home directory. That seems right since they will work without a conf file once they have a directory. It seems to also work if `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, even if set to the same unreachable `/var/empty/newuser`.

I would like `podman run` (and other commands which need the lock) to use somewhere in `graphRoot` by default to store this lock file, or fallback to somewhere in `graphRoot`, or allow the location to be configurable. I have tried `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and `XDG_DATA_HOME` but was unable to have `podman run` work without a writable home dir.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

This is an ostree distro

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I don't see us making `podman info` work in this case. It requires a fully-functional libpod to collect its information, which we cannot get without a writable $HOME. I would expect `podman version` to work, but that would be about the extent of it?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
